summary: Frontend testing of 3D flashcard flip feature. 9/10 requirements passing. 1 confirmed bug: audio button click also triggers card flip because React's e.stopPropagation() fires AFTER framer-motion's native pointerdown listener has already processed the event.

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking the audio button also triggers a card flip. React's `onPointerDown={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}` is insufficient because framer-motion attaches NATIVE DOM event listeners to the motion.div (parent), which fire BEFORE React's synthetic event system processes the child's stopPropagation call. Verified: JS programmatic `.click()` does NOT trigger flip (onClick stopPropagation works), but native pointer events (mousedown+mouseup) DO flip the card.

action_items: Fix audio button flip issue: Replace `onPointerDown={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}` with a native event listener approach. The fix is to add a native pointerdown listener via useRef+useEffect on each audio button that calls `e.stopPropagation()` natively — this fires BEFORE framer-motion's listener on the parent. Example fix: `useEffect(() => { const btn = ref.current; if (!btn) return; const h = e => e.stopPropagation(); btn.addEventListener('pointerdown', h); return () => btn.removeEventListener('pointerdown', h); }, []);`
critical_code_review_comments: Flashcard.jsx: onPointerDown={e => e.stopPropagation()} on audio buttons does NOT prevent framer-motion from detecting a tap. React's synthetic event handlers (onPointerDown) execute AFTER native DOM listeners because React 17+ delegates to the root container. framer-motion adds native pointerdown listeners to the motion.div which fires BEFORE React processes the child's stopPropagation. Native event bubbling order: audio button → motion.div (framer-motion fires) → root (React fires). Fix: use useRef + useEffect with native addEventListener on each audio button.

details: Large Gurmukhi character displayed (96px)
Category label shown (e.g., CONSONANT, VOWEL CARRIER)
'tap to flip' hint at bottom
Audio button top-right with data-testid='audio-button'
Clicking card center flips to back face (rotateY 180deg)
CSS transition 0.26s cubic-bezier animation
Both faces present in DOM with backface-visibility:hidden
framer-motion onTap correctly distinguishes from drag
Small faded question text at top
Divider line
Large pink answer text (e.g., 'Ara' in pink-600)
Letter name below answer
'tap to flip back' hint at bottom
Audio button with data-testid='audio-button-back'

flip_back_to_front.details: Tapping card on back face flips back to rotateY(0deg)

details: Wrong/Correct buttons hidden before flip (revealed=false)
Wrong/Correct buttons appear after tap-flip (revealed=true)
Uses data-testid='wrong-btn' and 'correct-btn'
Swipe right (>80px) = Correct: advances card, updates score
Swipe left (>80px) = Wrong: advances card, updates score
Swipe without prior flip also works (card advances)

audio_button_no_flip.details: Audio button click via pointer events DOES trigger card flip. Root cause: React's e.stopPropagation() in onPointerDown fires after framer-motion's native listener. JS .click() (no pointer events) correctly does NOT flip the card.

details: After clicking Correct/Wrong button, card counter advances (1/10 → 2/10)
Next card starts at rotateY(0deg) = front face
Answer buttons hidden for new card (revealed=false)
key={currentCard.id} on Flashcard ensures component remounts
Session-length-20 button NOT disabled with only 5 letters
Start button shows '20 cards' when session-20 selected
useStudySession deck extension works: repeats letters to fill 20 cards
Study header shows '1/20'
sessionMultiplier=3 in SettingsContext
estimateDeckSize(5, {srEnabled:true, sessionMultiplier:3}) = max(5, round(5*3)) = 15
Start button shows 'Start Smart Review · ~15 cards' with SR enabled and 5 letters
Session-20 still not disabled in SR mode with 5 letters

spam_tapping.details: 6 rapid taps (50ms intervals) leave card in correct state (even taps = front face). No JS errors during spam tapping.

context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3D flip features work except audio button stopPropagation. The false-positive in earlier audio test (testing via text DOM presence) has been corrected. Reliable flip state check: page.evaluate to query inner div's style.transform. Audio button issue: React onPointerDown stopPropagation doesn't work with framer-motion native listeners. Fix needed: native addEventListener on audio button refs. All other features (front/back face content, flip animation, answer buttons, swipe gestures, session length, smart review estimate) are working correctly.

rca_of_issue.requirement: Audio button should NOT trigger card flip
rca_of_issue.root_cause: React's e.stopPropagation() in onPointerDown fires after framer-motion's native pointerdown listener on the parent motion.div. Event order: (1) pointerdown fires on audio button → (2) bubbles to motion.div → framer-motion's native listener registers 'tap start' → (3) bubbles to React root → React fires synthetic events → audio button's onPointerDown handler runs e.stopPropagation() — but step 2 already happened. React's onPointerDown is a synthetic event handled at the root container (React 17+), so it fires AFTER native listeners at intermediate DOM nodes.
rca_of_issue.evidence: JS programmatic btn.click() does NOT trigger flip (because click doesn't fire pointerdown). page.mouse.down() + page.mouse.up() DOES trigger flip (fires native pointerdown).
rca_of_issue.fix: Use native addEventListener on audio button DOM nodes (via useRef + useEffect) to call stopPropagation before framer-motion's listener runs: `btnRef.current.addEventListener('pointerdown', e => e.stopPropagation())`
